name: EXP-003 five-case genetic circuit design benchmark
description: Fixed natural-language design tasks for the Phase 6-lite reproducibility harness.

tasks.name: Two-input permissive reporter
tasks.request: Activate a reporter when input A or input B is present.
scoring_notes: Penalize polarity inversion or a missing input row.
Do not claim Cello mapping unless an external Cello run exists.

tasks.name: Set-reset memory toggle
tasks.request: Design a genetic toggle switch with SET and RESET inputs controlling a GFP output state.

required_behaviors: SET drives and latches the GFP state on.
RESET drives and latches the GFP state off.
The prior state is retained when SET and RESET are both absent.

scoring_notes: Evaluate state retention rather than only a static truth table.
Report unsupported behavior for simultaneous SET and RESET explicitly.

tasks.name: Three-node repressilator
tasks.request: Design a three-node repressilator with a fluorescent output that exhibits sustained oscillatory behavior.

required_behaviors: The simulated trace contains repeated peaks after the initial transient.
Oscillation amplitude and period are reported with their evaluation window.
Damped or non-oscillatory traces are not labeled as sustained oscillation.

scoring_notes: Use temporal evidence; a cyclic diagram alone is insufficient.

tasks.name: Cello-style A AND NOT B logic
tasks.request: Activate GFP only when input A is present and input B is absent.
scoring_notes: Penalize loss of the B repression condition.
Treat direct topology simulation as not mapped when Cello is not run.

tasks.name: Underspecified stress-response request
tasks.request: Design a genetic circuit that responds to stress and produces a useful output.
scoring_notes: A clarification response is the expected successful outcome.
Penalize invented stress sensors, outputs, hosts, or biological guarantees.
